name: Vega de Espinareda and Mining Basins Loop
name_original: El Bierzo – circular por Vega de Espinareda y Berlanga
description: This difficult 20.1-mile touring cycling route in El Bierzo offers stunning natural beauty and historical landmarks like the Roman Bridge.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Vega De Espinareda are set within the Los Ancares Leoneses Biosphere Reserve, a region defined by its mid and high-mountain landscapes. The area features extensive heathlands, ancient chestnut groves, and forests of oak and birch, carved by rivers such as the Cúa, Ancares, and Burbia. These routes often include challenging ascents into the Ancares Mountains, an extension of the Cantabrian mountain range. Best touring cycling routes around Vega De Espinareda The most popular touring cycling route is La Mirada Circular Nature Trail, a 37.5 miles (60.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 44 minutes to complete. This demanding route traverses the Los Ancares Leoneses Biosphere Reserve. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vega de Espinareda and Mining Basins Loop, a difficult 20.1 miles (32.4 km) path. This route features natural beauty and historical sites like the Roman Bridge. Local touring cyclists also love the Pereda de Ancares loop from Ermida da Madalena, a 29.7 miles (47.8 km) trail leading through the Ancares Mountains, often completed in about 3 hours 18 minutes. Touring cycling around Vega De Espinareda is defined by mid and high-mountain landscapes, river valleys, and extensive forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging ascents to routes on mostly paved surfaces. The routes in Vega De Espinareda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Vega De Espinareda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Vega De Espinareda?
A: There are nearly 50 touring cycling routes around Vega De Espinareda listed on komoot. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with 4 easy, 6 moderate, and 37 difficult options, many featuring challenging ascents into the Ancares Mountains.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 4 easy touring cycling routes around Vega De Espinareda. While the region is known for its challenging mountain terrain, these easier options provide a gentler introduction to the area's natural beauty. Look for routes with less elevation gain and shorter distances.

Q: What are some of the most challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pereda de Ancares – Puerto de Ancares loop from Fabero&lt;/a&gt; stand out. This route covers 64 km with over 1,500 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032886105/la-mirada-circular-nature-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mirada Circular Nature Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 60.4 km long with 1,392 meters of climbing, offering an immersive experience in the Los Ancares Leoneses Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Vega De Espinareda are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035404208/vega-de-espinareda-and-mining-basins-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vega de Espinareda and Mining Basins Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38146296" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pereda de Ancares loop from Ermida da Madalena&lt;/a&gt;. These loops are great for exploring the diverse landscapes without retracing your steps.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes often traverse the Los Ancares Leoneses Biosphere Reserve, featuring extensive heathlands, ancient chestnut groves, and forests of oak and birch. You might encounter rivers like the Cúa, Ancares, and Burbia. Notable natural attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3571085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantejeira Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2967876" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Didactic Forest of El Bierzo&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also home to diverse wildlife, including the Cantabrian brown bear and Iberian wolf, though sightings are rare.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest accessible by touring bike?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035404208/vega-de-espinareda-and-mining-basins-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vega de Espinareda and Mining Basins Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, passes by historical sites like the Roman Bridge over the Cúa River. You can also find traditional architecture, including pallozas, mills, and ancient churches, in the villages along many routes.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Vega De Espinareda?
A: The diverse landscapes of Vega De Espinareda, with its mid and high-mountain terrain, make spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling. The weather is generally milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the higher altitudes, but be prepared for warmer temperatures in the valleys. Winter cycling is possible but may be challenging due to snow and colder conditions in the mountains.

Q: Are there places to rest or picnic along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several recreational areas perfect for a break. You'll find 'merenderos' (picnic areas) like Merendero Corral de los Lobos and Merendero Corral de las Cabras, which are ideal spots to rest and enjoy the natural surroundings. The Merendero de Espinareda provides privileged views of the Cúa river valley.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Vega De Espinareda?
A: The touring cycling routes in Vega De Espinareda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the region's stunning natural beauty, the challenging yet rewarding ascents, and the immersive experience within the Los Ancares Leoneses Biosphere Reserve.

Q: How long do the touring cycling routes typically take to complete?
A: The duration varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. For example, the demanding &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032886105/la-mirada-circular-nature-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mirada Circular Nature Trail&lt;/a&gt;, at 60.4 km, typically takes around 4 hours and 44 minutes. Shorter, less challenging routes will naturally take less time, while longer, more strenuous ones like the 64 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pereda de Ancares – Puerto de Ancares loop from Fabero&lt;/a&gt; can take over 4 hours and 49 minutes.

Q: Is public transport available to access touring cycling routes in Vega De Espinareda?
A: While Vega De Espinareda is a smaller town, local bus services might connect it to larger hubs in the El Bierzo region. However, direct public transport access to specific trailheads within the Ancares Mountains can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to Vega De Espinareda and plan your route accordingly, considering that many routes start directly from the town or nearby villages.

Q: Are there any routes that offer particularly scenic viewpoints?
A: Many routes in the Los Ancares Leoneses Biosphere Reserve offer breathtaking views of the mid and high-mountain landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31434832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Didactic Forest of El Bierzo – View of Fabero loop from Fabero&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is designed to provide scenic vistas. Routes climbing into the Ancares Mountains will also reward cyclists with panoramic views of the valleys carved by the Cúa, Ancares, and Burbia rivers.
tips: Small town of Berciano known for its intriguing cave paintings, between 2,000 and 5,000 years old. Although the drawings are difficult to interpret, both human and animal forms (possibly dogs, deer or goats) are perceived, as well as some plant motifs.

Nestled in the foothills of the Sierra de Ancares, to the north of El Bierzo, Vega de Espinareda is surrounded by a natural environment that was declared a Biosphere Reserve in 2006. still cross an impressive Roman bridge.

The name of Berlanga comes from ancient Asturian languages in which "berg" meant 'citadel' and "lágara" meant 'elevated area'. This interpretation accompanies the idea that the municipality was formed by various Asturian villages on top of the hills, which today are the five towns that make up the municipality.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The El Bierzo – circular by Vega de Espinareda y Berlanga route is approximately 32.4 kilometers long with a significant elevation gain. For a touring cyclist with very good fitness, it typically takes around 2 hours and 35 minutes to complete.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as a 'difficult' touring bicycle ride, demanding 'very good fitness'. With 32.4 kilometers and about 740 meters of elevation gain, it's best suited for experienced cyclists looking for a challenging experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is mostly paved, offering a smooth ride for touring bikes. However, be prepared for potentially unpaved and rougher sections, especially in the broader El Bierzo region. A robust touring bike or a gravel bike would be better equipped to handle any varied surfaces you might encounter.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: As you cycle, you'll pass through charming locations and historical sites. Notable highlights include the Sésamo Village and Historic Churches, the historic Roman Bridge of Vega de Espinareda, and the village of Berlanga del Bierzo. The route also offers impressive landscapes of valleys covered with oak and chestnut trees.

faqs.question: Are there any other cycling trails that intersect with this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Transcantábrica, Ruta de las Cuencas Mineras del Carbón, Camino de Vega de Espinareda, and Puente Romano, among others.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this trail?
faqs.answer: The El Bierzo region, nestled in the foothills of the Sierra de Ancares, offers beautiful scenery. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant weather for cycling, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be warm, while winter may bring colder conditions and potential for snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Is the El Bierzo – circular by Vega de Espinareda y Berlanga route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas and villages, specific regulations for dogs on this particular cycling route are not detailed. As a general rule, if you bring your dog, ensure it is well-behaved, kept on a leash, and that you clean up after it. Always respect local signage and wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Reserva de la Biosfera de los Ancares Leoneses?
faqs.answer: The El Bierzo – circular by Vega de Espinareda y Berlanga route is located within the Reserva de la Biosfera de los Ancares Leoneses. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public trails within Biosphere Reserves in Spain. However, always adhere to responsible outdoor practices and respect any local regulations or protected area guidelines you may encounter.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the El Bierzo circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Vega de Espinareda. You should be able to find public parking within the village of Vega de Espinareda to begin your ride. Look for designated parking areas or consult local tourism information upon arrival for the most convenient spots.

faqs.question: What kind of natural environment and wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The route traverses a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, offering unspoiled natural landscapes. You'll cycle through valleys rich with oak and chestnut trees. The diverse natural environment provides opportunities to observe various local fauna, so keep an eye out for birds and other wildlife native to the region.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through villages like Vega de Espinareda and Berlanga del Bierzo, you can expect to find local cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation options. These villages offer opportunities to refuel and rest during or after your ride.
